title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Recovery Case: Gita Bisect Search Invariant
summary: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita bisect search invariant increment in reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Bisect Search Invariant
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G19 is a theory in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Bisect Search Invariant contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.

content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Bisect Search Invariant while producing reproducible bisect harness and causal report.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G19 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe. Competency probe: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful. A defensible solution applies Gita Bisect Search Invariant, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible bisect harness and causal report.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-bisect-search-invariant — Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Bisect tests commits in timestamp order and always needs exactly log2(n) trials. This fails the Gita Bisect Search Invariant contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Bisect Search Invariant evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bisect-search-invariant-106yc5" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bisect Search Invariant into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bisect-search-invariant-106yc5
# scenario-key: trace-106yc5 probe-5cy601 boundary-6yc510 owner-106yc5x fault-106yc5z
# before-ref: main=a11ce10 candidate=b00b1c5
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce10 candidate=c0ffee5
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
